fieldRegistry.label: Primary Reason For Home Health / Medical Necessity

fieldGroups.label: Medical Necessity And Homebound
fieldGroups.description: Narratives and evidence for home health need and homebound status.

fieldGroups.label: Diagnosis And Coding
fieldGroups.description: Diagnosis evidence, coding-sensitive rows, and escalation flags.

fieldGroups.label: Medications, Allergies, And Special Treatments
fieldGroups.description: Medication safety, allergies, injectables, and O0110 special treatments.

fieldGroups.label: Functional And Therapy Status
fieldGroups.description: GG/M functional scoring, therapy need, discipline frequency, and goals.

fieldGroups.label: Symptom And Body System Review
fieldGroups.description: Body-system review sections that QA checks for consistency and completeness.

fieldGroups.label: Risk And Safety Assessments
fieldGroups.description: Fall, wound, hospitalization, and safety-related risk tools.

fieldGroups.label: Care Plan And Coordination
fieldGroups.description: Plan-of-care orders, patient summary, skilled interventions, and care plan content.

fieldGroups.label: Supplementals And History
fieldGroups.description: Past medical history, caregiver support, living situation, immunizations, and supplementals.

fieldGroups.label: Low Priority Admin Reference
fieldGroups.description: Identity, agency, physician, and demographic reference values that should not dominate QA.

chartSnapshot.primary_reason_for_home_health_medical_n: The patient is considered homebound because she has significant functional limitations and medical conditions that make leaving her home difficult and unsafe without assistance.
chartSnapshot.homebound_narrative: The patient is considered homebound because she has significant functional limitations and medical conditions that make leaving her home difficult and unsafe without assistance.
chartSnapshot.homebound_supporting_factors: Dyspnea with minimal exertion, Generalized weakness, Speech, Legally blind
chartSnapshot.living_situation: The patient requires assistance of another person to leave the home due to her multiple comorbidities, limited mobility, muscle weakness, and safety risks associated with falls and functional decline.
chartSnapshot.prior_functioning: The patient required maximal assistance for mobility and activities of daily living during her recent hospitalization but progressed to ambulating with a walker.
chartSnapshot.functional_limitations: Dyspnea with minimal exertion, Generalized weakness, Speech, Legally blind
chartSnapshot.therapy_need: The patient requires ongoing skilled therapy for rehabilitation and medical management due to her multiple comorbidities, limited mobility, muscle weakness, and safety risks associated with falls and functional decline.
chartSnapshot.patient_summary_narrative: The patient is a 69-year-old female with a history of a minimally displaced fracture of the left inferior pubic ramus and left pubic symphysis due to a mechanical fall, resulting in impaired mobility and requiring use of a wheelchair and walker for ambulation. She has generalized muscle weakness and other abnormalities of gait and mobility, compounded by morbid obesity, chronic kidney disease, chronic heart failure with reduced ejection fraction (35%), and a history of recurrent mechanical falls. These conditions c
chartSnapshot.respiratory_status: The patient has dyspnea with minimal exertion and requires ongoing skilled therapy for rehabilitation and medical management.
chartSnapshot.pain_assessment_narrative: The patient experiences chronic left shoulder pain and tremor, fine motor decline, and speech difficulty, all impairing her physical function.
chartSnapshot.emotional_behavioral_status: The patient is alert, oriented, forgetful at times, able to communicate needs, able to follow instructions, cooperative, and verbally responsive. Patient experiences episodes of depression.
